steps.title: Track the yield trade-off
steps.content: The passage says taller corn can produce more ears, but taller stalks are also more likely to snap or be uprooted before harvest. The conclusion must explain why shorter corn could improve the amount actually harvested.

steps.title: Choose the harvest advantage
steps.content: The matching choice says shorter varieties could improve harvested yields by surviving severe winds. Choice B reverses the stability point, Choice C introduces land requirements not discussed, and Choice D contradicts the passage by claiming shorter plants would produce more ears. The answer is A.
